title: anthurium citrifolium
intro: Anthurium citrifolium, also known as the velvet card or bird's nest anthurium, is a species of flowering plant in the arum family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in its roots, which are said to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Anthurium citrifolium are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots are often dried and made into teas or infusions, or used in tinctures and ointments to harness their medicinal properties.
